about.heroTitle: Born on the job site, not in a boardroom
about.heroLead: Noemí was not born in a conference room, but by listening to the people who install shutters every day. Talking to electricians, mechanics, and renovators, we saw the same problem: management tools are built for accountants and offices, not for people who work with their hands on site.
about.slide0Alt: German Castillo: from construction work to code, creator of Noemí
about.slide1Alt: German Castillo on site with a phone: a professional quote in an instant
about.slide2Alt: German Castillo: from the job site to code, Valencia and Argentina
about.carouselTablistAria: Choose a photo from the story
about.founderQuote: “I understand your day-to-day because I was there: dust on your boots and you still have to send the quote.”
about.founderSectionTitle: From the job site to code
about.founderP1: Hi, I’m German Castillo, the creator of Noemí. Four years ago I arrived in Valencia from Argentina with a backpack full of dreams and drive. Construction welcomed me first in Spain: I worked as a bricklayer, painter, and laying parquet floors.
about.founderP2: I know what it’s like to finish an exhausting day and fight with a computer to send a quote instead of resting. That’s why I poured my experience and my passion for computing into a real solution. Noemí is not “just software”; it’s my thank-you to a sector that gave me everything when I arrived—and the most valuable thing I want to give back: your time.
about.pillarsIntro: Unlike tools built only for offices, Noemí is designed for professionals in the field: radical simplicity, mobile-first, and a promise not to waste your time on unnecessary complexity.
about.pillarsImageAlt: Preview of Noemí with key benefits for field professionals

preview.benefit1Desc: Create quotes in seconds. Fewer errors.
preview.benefit2Desc: Fast and professional invoicing. Total control.
preview.benefit3Desc: Access everything from wherever you are.

about.pillar1Body: Interface optimised for thumbs, not only for mice. Your office is wherever you are.
about.pillar2Body: JWT, encryption, and good practices working in the background. Your data stays yours.
about.pillar3Body: When you talk to Noemí, you talk to me. No bots or call centers—someone who speaks your language.
about.pillar4Body: Open your phone, add materials and services, and the client can get the PDF before you walk out the door.
about.pillar5Body: Invoicing and collections without unnecessary jargon: what matters, in plain sight.
about.pillar6Body: The platform grows with the real needs of self-employed workers in Spain, without losing its simple core.
about.letterP1: I know exactly what it feels like to finish a ten-hour day on site with a sore back and your mind still on the next quote. I know what it’s like to get home in Valencia after heat or cold and open the computer because you “have to invoice” to get paid for work you earned with sweat.
about.letterP2: Four years ago, when I arrived at 24, construction opened its doors and taught me the value of effort in this country. I worked hard as a bricklayer, painter, and parquet fitter, grateful for every opportunity. But while my hands worked the floor or the walls, my mind stayed connected to my big dream: computing.
about.letterP3: I watched my coworkers and saw that many big solutions didn’t speak our language: complex programs built for offices, not for running a business from a van or a job site.
about.letterP4: That’s why, now at 27, I built Noemí. It’s not just code; it’s my way of saying thank you. It’s the tool I would have wanted when I started. I built it on my own, step by step, with the same precision I learned laying parquet, so you don’t lose one more minute than necessary in front of the screen.
about.letterP5: My commitment is simple: Noemí will always stay easy, fast, and human. Because I understand your effort, I respect your trade, and I want your time to truly be yours when the job is done.
about.letterP6: Thank you for trusting a project born from effort and respect for work done well.
about.letterSignoffRole: Your teammate and developer of Noemí.
about.ctaText: Let’s win back your evenings together—one quote at a time.

acctCfg.title: Account settings
acctCfg.subtitlePrefix: Choose which emails we send. For language, currency and time zone open
acctCfg.loginEmailMuted: This is the account you sign in with. Changing it requires verification — contact support if you need help.
acctCfg.hintBilling: Receipts, failed payments, end of trial and plan-related notices.
acctCfg.hintDocs: Reminders when you send documents (when automated sending is enabled).
acctCfg.hintMarketing: Ideas to get more out of the app. No spam — at most occasional messages.

pwdChange.title: Change password
pwdChange.subtitle: Use a long, unique password. If you sign in with Google and don’t recall a password you can
pwdChange.subtitleTail: with your email to set one.
pwdChange.cardSubtitle: At least 8 characters. Don’t reuse your current password.
pwdChange.footnote: After changing password this session stays active. If you used the account on another device sign out there or change again if you suspect unauthorised access.

support.title: Contact support
support.lead: Describe your issue and attach screenshots or a short video if you like. The message is sent to the support team from your account.
support.subject: Subject
support.messagePh: What happened, steps to reproduce, error message, etc.
support.attachmentsHint: Images, video (e.g. MP4) or PDF. Up to 5 files; 25 MB per file.

acctProf.title: Your profile
acctProf.subtitle: This is how you appear in the app and emails the system sends. You can’t change your sign‑in email here.
acctProf.loadErr: We couldn’t load your data. Check your connection and try again.
acctProf.cardPersonalSub: Information you can update at any time
acctProf.emailHintRO: To change sign‑in email, contact support (email verification coming soon).
acctProf.dobFuture: Date cannot be in the future
acctProf.hintSecurity: Password and advanced security (2FA, sessions) are managed elsewhere in your user menu.

acctFiscal.title: Tax details
acctFiscal.subtitleLead: Information the app will use as a
acctFiscal.subtitleTrail: (regime, activity, intra‑EU VAT). Must match reality; contact your accountant if unsure.
acctFiscal.mirrorSub: Company name, tax ID and tax address shown as issuer on PDFs. Edit in “Company details”.
acctFiscal.regimenSub: Applied as defaults on new invoices (“Tax regime” field).
acctFiscal.activityHint: Useful for wording on documents; not a substitute for each invoice’s specifics.
acctFiscal.activityPh: e.g. Installation and servicing of HVAC
acctFiscal.vatIdHint: Only if you trade intra‑EU with reverse charge.
acctFiscal.footnote: This app isn’t fiscal advice; changing rules may need more fields later.

reg.EXENTO: Exempt (exempt transactions with no input VAT)
reg.NO_SUJETO: Out of VAT scope due to place-of-supply rules
reg.MODULES: Freelancer in modules (lump-sum scheme) — check with your accountant

acctTotp.title: Two‑factor authentication (2FA)
acctTotp.subtitle: Adds an extra step: besides your password (or Google) you enter a 6‑digit code from an app such as Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator or Authy.
acctTotp.activeSub: Your account requires a verification code.
acctTotp.activeHint: You’ll enter your app code on sign‑in. If you turn off 2FA, only email and password (or Google) are needed.
acctTotp.disableHint: For security enter your password and a valid app code.
acctTotp.enableSub: Scan the QR or enter the key manually.
acctTotp.pendingBanner: You have unfinished setup on the server. You can regenerate a QR (previous one stops working) or cancel.
acctTotp.expiry: This step expires in about {{min}} minutes. If it lapses generate a new QR.
acctTotp.qrBroken: Couldn’t generate the QR image. Use the manual key or a compatible app.
acctTotp.confirmHint: Enter the 6‑digit code from your app.

acctPay.title: Payment methods
acctPay.subtitleTrail: on invoices: default method, IBAN/Bizum, and reminders.
acctPay.cardInvoiceSub: Defaults when you create an invoice — you can still change each document.
acctPay.ibanErr: Invalid IBAN — check digits (Spain uses ES + 22 digits). Spaces are OK.
acctPay.ibanHint: Recommended if you use transfer. Stored without separators.
acctPay.remSubBefore: Email the customer on the timelines indicated
acctPay.remSubAfter: , if the invoice is still unpaid.
acctPay.remPick: Choose one or more days after due date:
acctPay.remFootLead: By default reminders use the application mail service; inbound SMTP setup isn’t needed. To use Gmail or Microsoft, change outbound mail under
acctPay.remFootTrail: . One email is sent per invoice and reminder slot. Customers without an email address are skipped.
acctPay.footnote: IBAN/Bizum are sent over HTTPS and only readable for your account.

acctTpl.title: Footer text on your PDFs
acctTpl.leadAfter: of estimates and invoices you download or email (terms, deadlines, thanks).
acctTpl.leadNext: The top of the PDF still shows your name, logo and details — change those on the screens linked below.
acctTpl.readonly: You can read the texts. An active subscription is required to save changes.
acctTpl.quickTitle: Change the rest of the document?
acctTpl.budgetSub: Footer text on estimate PDF. Line items are per estimate.
acctTpl.condSub: Tick the options you want on by default when you create a new estimate; you can still change them per document.
acctTpl.kwSub: Each button shows a simple name. Click it to copy its code — paste into the box above (right‑click → Paste or Ctrl+V). Hover to see an example value. The quick preview replaces placeholders with sample data; the app’s PDF still prints the raw codes for now.
acctTpl.previewPickDoc: Choose whether to preview a sample estimate or invoice.
acctTpl.modePdfHint: Same PDF the app generates (may take a moment).
acctTpl.footPlaceholder: — What you typed above appears here —
acctTpl.pdfPreviewFailDefault: Could not show the sample PDF.
acctTpl.placeholderBudget: Example: Valid 30 days. VAT is shown in the body.
acctTpl.placeholderInvoice: Example: Thank you for your trust. Call or email us with any question.

acctAudit.title: Access history
acctAudit.subUser: Logins and security‑sensitive actions. You only see your user.
acctAudit.subAdmin: As admin you see the whole organisation.
acctAudit.utcNote: Timestamps shown in UTC to match servers.
acctAudit.warnBody: In the last 24 h there are {{n}} failed visible events ({{m}} sensitive) in scope. Review failed logins and reasons.
acctAudit.foot: Audit rows are append‑only; retention is server‑policy. IPs may appear masked.

acctHelp.title: Help centre
acctHelp.subtitle: Quick answers and links. Still stuck? Reach out via support.
acctHelp.faqEmpty: Nothing for «{{q}}». Try other words or contact support.
acctHelp.moreText: Tell us promptly; for bugs explain what you were doing.
acctHelp.faq_email_pdf_a: Check spam. Verify customer email. If using SMTP verify credentials. Resend from the document.
acctHelp.faq_stripe_a: Valid card/funds and try incognito if needed. Share the gateway message/time with support.
acctHelp.faq_pdf_empty_a: Ensure lines exist and reload/regenerate PDF. Capture if it persists.
acctHelp.faq_pw_a: Use “Forgot password” on login — check spam. Google users can restore via email.
acctHelp.faq_invoice_num_a: IDs are normally unique — follow advisor rules for corrective invoices.
acctHelp.faq_iva_a: Toggle VAT per document; totals match. Confirm with your adviser.

acctSess.title: Active sessions
acctSess.lead: Each sign‑in logs browser/OS/IP for accountability. Close other devices without touching this browser.
acctSess.legacyWarn: This account uses a legacy login without session id.
acctSess.legacyWarnBold: Sign out and log in again
acctSess.legacyWarnTail: so this device shows with full metadata.
acctSess.empty: No active sessions logged. Sign in again to register this device.
acctSess.confirmCloseOthers: Sign out everywhere else? This browser session stays.

acctSub.title: Subscription & plan
acctSub.subtitle: See your account status, key dates, and secure links to pay or manage billing through Stripe.
acctSub.bannerROText: You can’t edit until billing is cleared or upgraded.
acctSub.summarySub: Refreshes on login and after payment.
acctSub.renewStopDd: Your subscription will not auto‑renew after the current term.
acctSub.payPendingDd: Stripe awaits the next billing step (e.g. bank auth).
acctSub.stripeHint: Stripe lets you switch monthly vs annual. Tax may calculate there.
acctSub.stripeCheckoutDetailed: In Stripe checkout you can choose monthly billing ({{mo}}) or yearly ({{yr}}). With Stripe Tax on, VAT is calculated there.
acctSub.stripeFoot: Payments & cards handled by Stripe; we don’t store card data.
acctSub.pctSave: Paying yearly saves roughly {{pct}} % versus 12 months at monthly price.
acctSub.hTrialEnjoy: Enjoy full features during the trial.
acctSub.hTrialEnded: Trial date passed — subscribe to keep editing.
acctSub.hTrialToday: Trial ends today — subscribe to keep full access.
acctSub.hActiveRenewOff: Active but renewal off — reopen billing portal to flip auto‑renew.
acctSub.hTrialing: Stripe trial — billing picks up afterward.
acctSub.hPastDue: Last payment failed — update billing method.
acctSub.hUnpaid: Multiple failed charges — settle in Stripe.

acctCo.title: Company details
acctCo.subtitle: Shows on budgets/invoices who you are and how clients reach you. Logo is in the PDF header.
acctCo.loadErr: Could not load configuration. Check connection.
acctCo.rubroHint: Platform stats only — not printed on invoices.
acctCo.logoSub: PNG, JPEG or WebP, up to about 380 KB. Shown beside titles on estimate and invoice PDFs.
acctCo.noLogoHint: No logo — PDF uses text header.
acctCo.smtpIntro: Legacy SMTP with username/password. Choose “Manual SMTP” above only if you must keep this method; for new accounts prefer app mail or OAuth.
acctCo.smtpPassHintStored: Leave blank to keep stored password.
acctCo.advancedIntro: By default mail is sent through the application service—you don’t need anything else. Optionally you can link Gmail or Microsoft.
acctCo.optSmtpLegacy: Manual SMTP (migration from old setups only)
acctCo.systemFromHint: If empty, server default sender applies.
acctCo.oauthWarnGoogle: Google OAuth is not configured. Add variables or app.email.google.* (see docs/EMAIL‑OAUTH‑SETUP.md).
acctCo.oauthWarnMs: Microsoft OAuth is not configured. Add app.email.microsoft.* (see docs).
acctCo.oauthDisconnected: Not connected — authorize with the provider button.
acctCo.oauthFailHint: When token expires or provider rejects submission.
